en: Some time after this, the cupbearer of the king of Egypt and his baker committed an offense against their lord the king of Egypt.
zh: 這些事以後，埃及王的酒政和膳長，得罪了他們的主人埃及王。

en: And Pharaoh was angry with his two officers, the chief cupbearer and the chief baker,
zh: 法老就惱怒酒政和膳長這兩個臣宰，

en: and he put them in custody in the house of the captain of the guard, in the prison where Joseph was confined.
zh: 把他們關在軍長府內的監房裡，就是約瑟被囚禁的地方。

en: The captain of the guard appointed Joseph to be with them, and he attended them. They continued for some time in custody.
zh: 軍長把他們交給約瑟，約瑟就侍候他們。他們在監房裡過了好些日子。

en: And one night they both dreamed- the cupbearer and the baker of the king of Egypt, who were confined in the prison- each his own dream, and each dream with its own interpretation.
zh: 他們二人，就是被關在監裡的埃及王的酒政和膳長，同一夜各作了一個夢，二人的夢各有不同的解釋。

en: When Joseph came to them in the morning, he saw that they were troubled.
zh: 到了早晨，約瑟進去到他們那裡，見他們神色不安，

en: So he asked Pharaoh's officers who were with him in custody in his master's house, "Why are your faces downcast today?"
zh: 就問法老的兩個臣宰，就是與他一同關在他主人府內的監房裡的，說：“今天你們的臉色為甚麼這樣難看呢？”

en: They said to him, "We have had dreams, and there is no one to interpret them." And Joseph said to them, "Do not interpretations belong to God? Please tell them to me."
zh: 他們回答他：“我們各人作了一個夢，但沒有人能夠解釋。”約瑟對他們說：“解夢不是出於 神嗎？請把夢告訴我吧。”

en: So the chief cupbearer told his dream to Joseph and said to him, "In my dream there was a vine before me,
zh: 酒政就把自己的夢告訴約瑟，對他說：“在夢裡我看見我面前有一棵葡萄樹。

en: and on the vine there were three branches. As soon as it budded, its blossoms shot forth, and the clusters ripened into grapes.
zh: 葡萄樹上有三根枝子；樹一發芽，就開了花，上頭的葡萄都成熟了。

en: Pharaoh's cup was in my hand, and I took the grapes and pressed them into Pharaoh's cup and placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand."
zh: 法老的杯在我手裡，我拿了葡萄擠在法老的杯中，把杯遞在法老的手裡。”

en: Then Joseph said to him, "This is its interpretation: the three branches are three days.
zh: 約瑟對他說：“這夢的解釋是這樣：三根枝子就是三天。

en: In three days Pharaoh will lift up your head and restore you to your office, and you shall place Pharaoh's cup in his hand as formerly, when you were his cupbearer.
zh: 三天之內，法老必使你抬起頭來，恢復你原來的職位；你仍要把杯遞在法老的手裡，好像先前作他的酒政時一樣。

en: Only remember me, when it is well with you, and please do me the kindness to mention me to Pharaoh, and so get me out of this house.
zh: 不過，你一切順利的時候，求你記念我，施恩給我，在法老面前提拔我，救我脫離這監獄。

en: For I was indeed stolen out of the land of the Hebrews, and here also I have done nothing that they should put me into the pit."
zh: 我實在是從希伯來人之地被拐來；就是在這裡，我也沒有作過甚麼事該被關在監牢裡的。”

en: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was favorable, he said to Joseph, "I also had a dream: there were three cake baskets on my head,
zh: 膳長見夢解得好，就對約瑟說：“我也作了一個夢，在夢裡我看見自己頭上有三籃白餅。

en: and in the uppermost basket there were all sorts of baked food for Pharaoh, but the birds were eating it out of the basket on my head."
zh: 最上面的籃子裡，有為法老烤的各種食物，但飛鳥來吃我頭上籃子裡的食物。”

en: And Joseph answered and said, "This is its interpretation: the three baskets are three days.
zh: 約瑟回答：“這夢的解釋是這樣：三個籃子就是三天。

en: In three days Pharaoh will lift up your head- from you!- and hang you on a tree. And the birds will eat the flesh from you."
zh: 三天之內，法老必砍下你的頭來，把你掛在木頭上，必有飛鳥來吃你身上的肉。”

en: On the third day, which was Pharaoh's birthday, he made a feast for all his servants and lifted up the head of the chief cupbearer and the head of the chief baker among his servants.
zh: 到了第三日，就是法老的生日，他為臣僕擺設筵席，在他們面前把酒政和膳長提出監來，

en: He restored the chief cupbearer to his position, and he placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.
zh: 恢復了酒政原來的職位，叫他可以把杯遞在法老的手中；

en: But he hanged the chief baker, as Joseph had interpreted to them.
zh: 法老卻把膳長掛起來，正如約瑟給他們的解釋一樣。

en: Yet the chief cupbearer did not remember Joseph, but forgot him.
zh: 但是酒政並不記念約瑟，竟把他忘記了。
